title: Publisher Buffers, Drain, Admission, and Bounded Batching · Inference Brief: Upstream Admission Budget
summary: A publisher awaits a boolean from publish, ignores false, buffers unbounded work during broker flow control, and batches until memory exhaustion while upstream requests continue to receive success. Planned artifact: Add and verify the upstream admission budget increment in publisher admission and buffer-pressure controller. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Propagate publisher capacity to HTTP or job admission with queue, byte, time, tenant, and rejection budgets.
Pause admission after false, coordinate a bounded drain waiter, handle close and abort, and avoid lost wakeups or listener leaks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Upstream Admission Budget · Theory Upstream Admission Budget
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 9822 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to propagate blocked, byte capacity, publish boolean, and drain state into upstream admission; retain duplicate-safe identity through every ambiguous handoff; then raise a broker resource alarm and stop new publisher admission. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Upstream Admission Budget contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Upstream Admission Budget · Theory Upstream Admission Budget.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for propagate publisher capacity to http or job admission with queue, byte, time, tenant, and rejection budgets. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Upstream Admission Budget · Theory Upstream Admission Budget, keep Upstream Admission Budget connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Build Upstream Admission Budget evidence · Theory Upstream Admission Budget
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: rabbit-ts-upstream-admission-budget-yufk13" that uses case 4404 to propagate blocked, byte capacity, publish boolean, and drain state into upstream admission. Browser code must not import amqplib or Node built-ins, open sockets, contact RabbitMQ, Docker, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the named Node 24, amqplib 2.0.1, RabbitMQ 4.3, TLS, partition, restart, load, upgrade, and production transfer gates.
